title: Bavli loci — witness-backed index
description: Curated passages with witness_delta: plain-English notes on Vilna vs manuscripts (for readers who do not read Hebrew).

denial_scale.0: Fully present / uncensored in target layer
denial_scale.3: Removed or heavily censored in Vilna-era print

tradition_status.ikkar: Presented as settled halacha or consensus
tradition_status.machloket: Explicit dispute between authorities in the text
tradition_status.maaseh: Reported event / baraita / narrative in the record

topic.en: Baraita on trial and execution of Yeshu ha-Notzri
topic.he: ברaita על דין ותליית יֵשׁוּ הַנּוֹצְרִי

loci.tradition_note: Historical-legal baraita inside a mesis/sanhedrin sugya. Gemara argues whether he had zechut — not Jewish creed about Christianity.

sources.label: Instone-Brewer — Jesus' Trial in the Uncensored Talmud

loci.english_summary: Trial and execution of Yeshu (Jesus). The gemara asks whether he might have deserved acquittal (had zechut / merit) — a legal argument inside a mesis case, not church doctrine about whether Jesus was OK.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: The baraita naming Yeshu ha-Notzri and describing his trial and execution was omitted entirely from the standard Vilna Shas and many pre-modern printings.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Munich 95 and Bomberg retain a baraita: Yeshu ha-Notzri was hanged on the eve of Passover; the gemara then asks whether he might have had zechut (legal merit) for acquittal — a procedural question inside the laws of mesis (enticer), not a creed about Christianity.
witness_delta.plain_english: The Talmud records a trial-and-execution narrative about a figure called Yeshu the Nazarene, then debates whether the court could have acquitted him on technical grounds. Jewish tradition treats this as a legal-historical report in context, not as normative theology about Jesus.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Sefaria’s Hebrew gemara often restores this passage; English may paraphrase softly. Compare their text to this build and to Instone-Brewer.

topic.en: ben Stada / ben Pandera — mesis execution report
topic.he: בן סטada / בן פndera — מעשה במesis

loci.tradition_note: Illustrative case in mesis procedure; amoraic wordplay on names — narrative within halachic framework.
loci.english_summary: Ben Stada / ben Pandera — reported execution of a mesis (enticer). Names linked in tradition to Yeshu passages elsewhere.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Passage linking ben Stada / ben Pandera to execution for mesis was cut from Vilna-era print.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Manuscripts report the execution of ben Stada from Lud; amoraic discussion plays on the names ben Stada and ben Pandera — traditionally cross-read with Yeshu loci.
witness_delta.plain_english: A reported capital case for ‘enticing’ (mesis), with wordplay on names later linked in rabbinic literature to the same figure cluster as Sanhedrin 43a.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Restoration varies by layer; read English alongside Munich-based Hebrew here.

topic.en: Yeshu ha-Notzri in Gehinnom / Onkelos necromancy narrative
topic.he: יֵשׁוּ הַנּוֹצְרִי בגיהinnom — aggadah in destruction context

loci.tradition_note: Aggadic passage on Roman destruction era; not halachic ruling or ikkar emunah.
loci.english_summary: Aggadic story: Onkelos summons Yeshu ha-Notzri from Gehinnom. Narrative in destruction-era context, not halacha.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Aggadic block naming Yeshu ha-Notzri among those punished in Gehinnom was removed from censored print.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Onkelos the convert summons Yeshu ha-Notzri from Gehinnom in an aggadic chain about Roman oppressors — homiletical narrative tied to the destruction era, not a halachic ruling.
witness_delta.plain_english: A story-world vignette: a named Yeshu the Nazarene appears in Gehinnom in a polemical aggadah about empire and punishment. It is legend in context, not law.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: English translations may tone down polemical edges; Hebrew witness text is the check.

topic.en: Yeshu as student of R. Yehoshua ben Perahiah
topic.he: יֵשׁוּ תalmid של ר' יהושע בן פרחיה

loci.tradition_note: Aggadic biography linking Yeshu to a sage; debated historicity — story, not halacha.
loci.english_summary: Aggadah: Yeshu as a student of R. Yehoshua ben Perahiah — biography story, debated historicity.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Biographical aggadah placing Yeshu as a student of R. Yehoshua ben Perahiah was censored from standard print.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Story of a student (named Yeshu in uncensored witnesses) who misunderstood his teacher’s hospitality and was expelled — moralistic aggadah with debated historicity.
witness_delta.plain_english: A cautionary tale about a brilliant but misguided student; later tradition names him Yeshu. Read as narrative ethics, not biography.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Name and details often absent in censored editions; Sefaria may restore Hebrew but English summaries vary.

topic.en: Parallel: Yeshu and R. Yehoshua ben Perahiah
topic.he: גרסה מקבילה — יֵשׁוּ ור' יהושע בן פרחיה

loci.tradition_note: Parallel aggadic tradition to Sanhedrin 107b; same witness profile.
loci.english_summary: Parallel aggadic tradition to Sanhedrin 107b — Yeshu and R. Yehoshua ben Perahiah.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Parallel version of the student narrative — same censorship profile as Sanhedrin 107b.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Duplicate aggadic tradition; uncensored witnesses preserve the Yeshu naming.
witness_delta.plain_english: Same story-line as 107b in a parallel sugya — useful for textual witness comparison.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Compare both dapim; do not assume English on one replaces the other.

topic.en: ben Stada / ben Pandera — sorcery narrative
topic.he: בן סטada — נarrative in magic/sorcery context

loci.tradition_note: Reported case in sorcery sugya; linked by tradition to Sanhedrin 67a identities.
loci.english_summary: Ben Stada in a sorcery narrative — linked by tradition to Sanhedrin 67a.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: ben Stada material in the sorcery context was suppressed in Vilna print.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Narrative in a magic/sorcery discussion preserving ben Stada / ben Pandera name-play tied to Sanhedrin 67a.
witness_delta.plain_english: Legal-aggadic overlap between sorcery law and the ben Stada name traditions.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Among the easier passages to miss in English-only browsing; Hebrew restoration matters.

topic.en: גוים restored where Vilna Rashi read עובדי כוכבים
topic.he: החזרת גוים במקום עובדי כוכבים — רש"י פתיחת מסכת

loci.tradition_note: Opening mishnah on commerce before gentile festivals — legal argument; dispute here is censored *language*, not the halacha itself.

sources.label: Waxman — Uncensored Printings for Avodah Zarah

loci.english_summary: Rashi restored to read gentiles (goyim) where Vilna print had the censored phrase idol-worshippers.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Vilna Rashi routinely reads עובדי כוכבים (idol-worshippers) where earlier witnesses have גוים (gentiles/nations).
witness_delta.witness_reading: Munich/Bomberg Rashi on the opening mishnah uses גוים — the direct word Rashi wrote before Christian censorship.
witness_delta.plain_english: Not a change in halacha but in vocabulary: ‘gentiles’ was replaced by the safer phrase ‘idol-worshippers’ in print. The legal sugya is about commerce before pagan festivals.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Sefaria English often follows Vilna Rashi wording — you may read ‘idol-worshippers’ where witnesses say ‘gentiles’.

topic.en: עכו"ם acronym in Rashi (should read גוים/מין)

loci.tradition_note: Rashi on Shema / minim — legal-exegetical point; עכו"ם is post-censor acronym, not original Rashi vocabulary.
loci.english_summary: Rashi uses the censor acronym akum where manuscripts read goyim or minim — language censorship in commentary.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Vilna Rashi uses עכו"ם (akum — censor acronym for ‘idol-worshippers’) instead of גוים or מין (sectarians) in Shema commentary.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Earlier Rashi witnesses read goyim or minim where Vilna has the opaque acronym עכו"ם.
witness_delta.plain_english: A censorship swap in commentary only: an invented acronym hides who Rashi was talking about in a legal reading of Shema.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: English will often say ‘idolaters’ or transliterate akum — it will not flag that Rashi’s word was different.

topic.en: Oven of Akhnai — bat kol vs human majority
topic.he: תנור של עכnai — בat kol מול רב majority

loci.tradition_note: Famous meta-dispute: halacha is not in heaven. Not censored — included as contrast (argument in text, not suppression).
loci.english_summary: Oven of Akhnai — famous dispute: law is not in heaven; human majority over bat kol. Not censored; shows argument on the record.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: No meaningful censorship — standard Vilna text matches witnesses.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Same as Vilna: Oven of Akhnai; Rabbi Eliezer vs sages; bat kol rejected — ‘It is not in heaven.’
witness_delta.plain_english: Famous dispute: human rabbinic majority outweighs a heavenly voice. Included as a baseline: rich argument preserved everywhere, not a suppression locus.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Sefaria English is a reliable reading aid here.

topic.en: Why halacha does not follow Rabbi Meir — dispute on record

loci.tradition_note: Meta-dispute about following a sage whose full reasoning peers could not grasp. Not censored — shows argument in the text without suppression.
loci.english_summary: Why halacha does not follow Rabbi Meir — meta-dispute about a sage peers could not fully follow. Not censored.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: No censorship — dispute about why halacha does not follow Rabbi Meir is on the record in all prints.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Same: peers could not fully grasp Rabbi Meir’s reasoning; meta-machloket on authority.
witness_delta.plain_english: Argument about limits of following a genius sage — preserved openly, not suppressed.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Safe to use Sefaria English for overview.

topic.en: Adam created single — universal human dignity teaching
topic.he: לפיכך נברא אדם יחידי — כבוד הבריות

loci.tradition_note: Often cited in interfaith ethics; present everywhere — baseline row (not censored).
loci.english_summary: Adam created alone — teaching on universal human dignity. Widely present; not a censorship locus.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Universal dignity teaching (‘Adam was created alone…’) appears in all standard editions.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Identical witness stream — teaching on human equality and murder accountability.
witness_delta.plain_english: Because God created one Adam, no person may say ‘my lineage is greater than yours’ — classic aggadah, uncensored.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: English translations align well; included as non-censorship contrast.

loci.tradition_note: Pattern row — not one daf. Censor acronym replacing גוים across commentary; see censorship_audit.json.
loci.english_summary: Corpus-wide pattern: akum acronym in Rashi/Tosafot (~100 places) replacing goyim — see censorship audit.

witness_delta.vilna_standard: Roughly ~100 places in Rashi/Tosafot where Vilna has עכו"ם instead of גוים or related direct terms.
witness_delta.witness_reading: Pattern across commentary layers: censor acronym replaces plain ‘gentiles’ in manuscript Rashi/Tosafot.
witness_delta.plain_english: 100 indexed commentary rows now have English witness notes — open the Audit list (audit.html) to browse every hit by tractate and daf, or open any flagged daf for inline notes.
witness_delta.sefaria_note: Default Sefaria commentary export reflects Vilna; use audit.html + per-daf notes here instead of trusting English commentary alone.
